display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V4_Temp_0.7/test31/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 0 -9.81" integrator="Euler" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <joint name="bob_joint" type="free"/>
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <equality>
    <!-- Energy computation can be enabled through sensors or by checking state -->
  </equality>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: using a geom with mass 0. Note: MuJoCo geoms typically have mass if they have density, but we can specify mass=0 if supported or just use a very small mass or rely on the bob for physics. However, the prompt asks for a massless rod. -->
      <geom name="rod" type="cylinder" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <sensor>
    <jointpos joint="hinge"/>
  </sensor>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.005" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <!-- Initial conditions: Pendulum starts horizontal (+x), angle +90 degrees from vertical (upward is +y) -->
  <!-- In MuJoCo, joint position is often an angle. To set initial state, we use <keyframe> -->
  <keyframe>
    <qpos name="horizontal" obj="hinge" vec="0.2485 0 0"/> 
    <!-- Wait, qpos for a hinge joint is the angle. 
         The hinge is at (0,1,0), axis (0,0,1). 
         Vertical is +y (0,1,0). 
         Horizontal (+x) is (0.2485, 1, 0) relative to world or (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to hinge.
         If vertical is 0, then horizontal (+x) is 90 degrees (PI/2) if the axis is (0,0,1) and gravity is (0,-9.81,0)? 
         Actually, if gravity is -y, vertical is +y. 
         A hinge on z-axis. 
         Angle 0 = vertical. 
         Angle PI/2 = horizontal.
    -->
    <keyframe qpos="1.5708" /> 
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 3 0" dir="0 -1 0"/>
    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.005" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <keyframe qpos="1.570796" />
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 3 0" dir="0 -1 0"/>
    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>

      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">

      </body>
    </body>

  <keyframe>

  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.005" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">

    <body name="pendulum_link" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.005" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
